clusters.leadHeadline: NWSL Adds 18th Team in Columbus for $205M
clusters.summary: Haslam Sports Group purchases Columbus expansion rights for $205M, marking NWSL's continued growth strategy and franchise valuation increases in women's soccer.

clusters.leadHeadline: Bears Stadium Plan Advances in Illinois Amid Relocation Pressure
clusters.summary: Illinois lawmakers rush to finalize $5B stadium financing as Bears explore Indiana relocation, forcing state decision on landmark infrastructure investment.

clusters.leadHeadline: NFL Top Pick Salaries Exceed $50M for First Time Since 2010
clusters.summary: 2026 first overall pick will earn nearly $55M, reflecting salary cap inflation and competitive bidding for elite talent in rookie compensation structures.

clusters.leadHeadline: NIL 'Associated Entities' Dispute Could Eliminate College Salary Cap
clusters.summary: Legal challenge over NIL regulation definition threatens to dismantle NCAA compensation rules, potentially creating unrestricted athlete spending landscape.

clusters.leadHeadline: Churchill Downs Acquires Preakness Stakes IP Rights for $85M
clusters.summary: Kentucky Derby owner consolidates Triple Crown media control with Preakness acquisition, centralizing racing IP and broadcast rights value.

clusters.leadHeadline: Athlete-Owned Media Platforms Gain Traction Post-Career
clusters.summary: Brandon Marshall's I AM ATHLETE and Adam Thielen's youth sports ventures show athletes diversifying into media and talent development businesses beyond playing.

poll.question: Will the NIL regulation loophole collapse college sports salary caps entirely?
options: Partial—only P5 conferences lose cap enforcement
No clue—the legal battle is too complex
